title: Indirect Proximity Ligation Assay (PLA) - Fluoresence
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7d05h","text":"Indirect Proximity Ligation Assay (PLA) is a powerful molecular technique used to detect and visualize protein-protein interactions, protein modifications, and protein complex formations within cells or tissues. This method is based on the principles of proximity-dependent ligation and utilizes specific antibodies to detect the nitration of proteins on free-floating brain sections. Here we describe the PLA protocol that we routinely use in our laboratory to detect nitrated alpha-synuclein and nitration of mitochondrial enzymes such as SOD2 and the mitochondrial complex 1 subunit NDUFB8.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"g2l0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85dpn","text":"\n\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pick 35um cut brain sections and transfer them to 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes: Note: all incubation and wash steps are performed by shaking Eppendorf tubes at 250rpm (e.g., thermomixer)
Wash 3x 00:05:00 with wash buffer A (see materials)
Antigen-retrieval with citrate buffer (+tween, pH = 6) at 95°C for 00:04:00 Note: Heat the solution to 95°C before adding on the samples
Blocking: Incubate in Duolink Blocking Solution ( 300 µL )at 37°C for 01:00:00

Primary Antibody Incubation : Dilute antibodies in Duolink Antibody Diluent ( 200 µL ) and incubate Overnight room temperature. for nitrated alpha-synuclein: mouse anti-3-NT and rabbit anti-h-alpha-synuclein for nitrated SOD2: mouse anti-3-NT and rabbit anti-SOD2 for nitrated NDUFB8: mouse anti-3-NT and rabbit anti-NDUFB8 see materials for dilutions and catalog numbers
PLA probe incubation :Prepare anti-mouse MINUS and anti-rabbit PLUS probes following manufacturer's instructions, add solution on sections and incubate at 37°C for 01:30:00

Ligation: Dilute the Duolink Ligation Buffer 1:5 in high-purity water Add the ligase (diluted 1:40) just before incubation (keep cold on freezer block!) add the ligation solution on sections and incubate at 37°C for 01:15:00

Amplification : This step is light-sensitive! incubation is performed using aluminum foil to protect samples from light. Dilute the Duolink Amplification Buffer (red) 1:5 in high-purity water Add the polymerase (diluted 1:80) just before incubation (keep cold on freezer block!) Add the amplification solution to the sections and incubate at 37°C for 02:30:00
Wash 2x 00:05:00 with wash buffer B Wash 1x 00:01:00 with wash 0.1x buffer B

Additional immunohistochemical staining (optional): if immunohistochemical counter-staining is going to be performed it is important to avoid the use of detergents in wash buffers and perform long incubations in cold.
Mount the samples on slides and let them air-dry for 5 mins
Coverslip samples with Duolink In Situ Mounting Medium with DAPI, store samples in the dark at 4°C
creator: Ayse Ulusoy
